title: Variables

concepts.title: What is a Variable?
concepts.content: A variable is a named reference used to store data in memory. Variables allow programs to store, retrieve, and manipulate values during execution.

concepts.title: Dynamic Typing
concepts.content: Python is dynamically typed. The type of a variable is determined automatically at runtime, and no explicit declaration is required.

concepts.title: Variable Naming Rules
concepts.content: Variable names can contain letters, digits, and underscores. They cannot start with a digit and cannot use Python reserved keywords.

concepts.title: Multiple Assignment
concepts.content: Python allows assigning multiple variables in a single statement.

concepts.title: Memory Reference Concept
concepts.content: Variables do not store actual values directly. They reference objects stored in memory.

interviewQuestions.question: What is a variable in Python?
interviewQuestions.answer: A variable is a named reference that points to an object stored in memory.

interviewQuestions.question: Is Python statically typed or dynamically typed?
interviewQuestions.answer: Python is dynamically typed because variable types are determined at runtime.

interviewQuestions.question: How do you check the type of a variable?
What is the difference between type() and isinstance()?
interviewQuestions.answer: type() checks the exact type, whereas isinstance() also considers inheritance relationships.

interviewQuestions.question: Can a variable change its type during execution?
interviewQuestions.answer: Yes. Python allows reassignment to objects of different types.

scenarioQuestions.question: You receive test data from an API and are unsure whether a field contains an integer or string. How would you validate it?
scenarioQuestions.answer: Use isinstance() to verify the data type before processing.

scenarioQuestions.question: A Selenium framework stores browser names dynamically. Why are variables useful here?
scenarioQuestions.answer: Variables allow values such as browser names, URLs, and credentials to be reused throughout the framework.

scenarioQuestions.question: During debugging, a variable unexpectedly changes type from integer to string. How would you identify the issue?
scenarioQuestions.answer: Print the variable value and type at different execution points using type() or logging.

codingQuestions.question: Declare and print variables of different data types.
Swap two variables without using a third variable.
Find the type of a variable.

realTimeExamples.title: Environment URL Storage
realTimeExamples.description: Frameworks store QA, UAT, and PROD URLs in variables.

realTimeExamples.title: Test Data Management
realTimeExamples.description: Usernames, passwords, patient IDs, and order numbers are stored in variables.

realTimeExamples.title: API Testing
realTimeExamples.description: Response values are stored in variables and reused across requests.

realTimeExamples.title: Automation Framework Configuration
realTimeExamples.description: Browser type, execution mode, timeout values, and report paths are stored in variables.

commonMistakes.mistake: Using Python keywords as variable names.
commonMistakes.fix: Avoid names such as class, def, if, else, while, etc.
Use meaningful names such as patient_id instead of p.
Prefer isinstance() for type validation because it supports inheritance.

quickRevision: Variables can change type at runtime
